title: Choose Instructions, Skills, Plugins, Hooks, or MCP · Threat Case: Plugin Fit
summary: Route recurring agent needs to the smallest durable extension surface. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext plugin fit increment in an extension-surface decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit.
Use MCP when live tools or external context must cross the agent-host boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Predict Plugin Fit
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Plugin Fit contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit.

content.text: Use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Plugin Fit, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an extension-surface decision matrix.

content.text: Every personal skill requires a plugin package. This fails the Plugin Fit contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Plugin Fit evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-plugin-fit-521dn2: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Plugin Fit into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-plugin-fit-521dn2:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

stimulus.title: Plugin Fit failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.

steps.title: Defend the Plugin Fit model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Plugin Fit applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit.
